valid.name: interval
valid.code: CREATE TABLE jobs (id int, duration interval);

valid.name: timestamptz
valid.code: CREATE TABLE shifts (id int, start_at timestamptz);

invalid.name: time
invalid.code: CREATE TABLE shifts (id int, start_at time);

errors.message: `TIME` and `TIME WITH TIME ZONE` rarely model anything correctly: `time` has no date so cannot disambiguate around DST, and `timetz` stores an offset that is meaningless without a date. Use `timestamptz` for points in time, `interval` for durations, or store an opaque `text` if all you need is a display value.

invalid.name: timetz
invalid.code: CREATE TABLE shifts (id int, start_at timetz);

errors.message: `TIME` and `TIME WITH TIME ZONE` rarely model anything correctly: `time` has no date so cannot disambiguate around DST, and `timetz` stores an offset that is meaningless without a date. Use `timestamptz` for points in time, `interval` for durations, or store an opaque `text` if all you need is a display value.
